summary: This cluster focuses on the taxonomic revision and phylogenetic reclassification of red algal genera within the family Delesseriaceae, specifically merging the cryptic genera *Radicilingua* and *Calonitophyllum*. Using molecular phylogenetic analysis of the rbcL-5P gene sequence alongside morphological data, the research established the genetic distinctiveness of these taxa and proposed the transfer of *Calonitophyllum medium* to *Radicilingua* to reflect evolutionary relationships. A new species, *Radicilingua mediterranea*, was described from the Mediterranean Sea, incorporating previously recognized specimens from that region. The findings highlight the utility of molecular markers in resolving long-standing taxonomic ambiguities among morphologically similar, geographically restricted marine algae.
